{"id":"project-LICHEN-rke6","title":"payload_fmt.py: _try_utf8 incorrectly accepts binary data with high printable ratio","description":"File: python/src/lichen/interface/kiss/payload_fmt.py
Lines: 64-77

The _try_utf8 function uses a 90% printable threshold:

```python
def _try_utf8(data: bytes) -> str | None:
    try:
        text = data.decode('utf-8')
        printable = sum(1 for c in text if c.isprintable() or c in '\n\r\t')
        if printable >= len(text) * 0.9:  # 90% printable
            return text
    except UnicodeDecodeError:
        pass
    return None
```

The 90% threshold is too permissive:
1. A 10-byte payload with 9 printable chars + 1 control char (e.g., null byte) passes
2. Binary protocols that happen to use mostly ASCII-range bytes could be misdetected
3. Structured binary data (e.g., protobuf with string fields) often has high printable ratio

Impact: Binary payloads misidentified as text, displayed incorrectly in TNC apps.

Fix: Either:
1. Require 100% printable + whitespace for text classification
2. Or add additional heuristics (check for common binary markers, require minimum length, etc.)
3. Or accept the tradeoff and document it","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:19:28.528871Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:19:28.528915Z"}
